channel.title: Design for Manufacture
channel.description: Essays on engineering, technology, and communist politics

item.title: The Immortal Science: Preface to the French Translation of “Forest and Factory”
Forest and Factory: The Science and the Fiction of Communism
item.description: This essay was written with my friend Phil Neel in order to try and answer the question of how communism might actually work. We find existing attempts to do this largely unsatisfactory, chiefly for their refusal to engage with the material-productive reality of this world and the constraints this reality poses on building a new world.

item.title: Engineers, Materialism, and the Communist Method
item.description: My new essay Engineers, Materialism, and the Communist Method can now be read at Notes From Below.

item.title: Red May 2023: We Have Never Been Post-Industrial
item.description: I recently organized a panel talk with my friends and collaborators Wendy Liu, Phil Neel, and Anette Ramos. You can watch the archived talk and subsequent Q&A here. Special thanks to the Red May organizers for facilitating all of this.
